name: Bicarbonate
mechanisms: Buffers lactic acid and delays fatigue.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.

claimMap.claim: v8.2: Sodium bicarbonate has sports-performance evidence, but dosing is sodium-heavy and GI-limited.
claimMap.notes: v8.2: Sodium bicarbonate has sports-performance evidence, but dosing is sodium-heavy and GI-limited.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: 0.2–0.3 g/kg sodium bicarbonate pre-exercise is commonly studied

raw_mechanisms: Buffers lactic acid and delays fatigue.
unmapped_mechanisms: Buffers lactic acid and delays fatigue.
safety: Generally well tolerated for most users.

sources.title: PubMed PMID 34503527. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
sources.citation: PubMed PMID 34503527. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
sources.note: evidence citation lookup; PMID source present in Evidence_Register

contraindications: Hypertension or heart failure or edema (sodium load can worsen fluid retention)
impaired kidney function or metabolic alkalosis (contraindicated due to altered acid-base and electrolyte handling)
ergogenic loading doses (dose-dependent GI upset such as bloating or cramping or diarrhea or vomiting)
drugs cleared by the kidney based on urine pH such as amphetamines or salicylate medications like aspirin (alters excretion and warrants dosing separation)
